title: Decision Assurance Case — frontmatter and structured assurance block
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a Decision Assurance Case (DAC), per spec-decision-assurance.md §5, plus the optional structured `assurance` block for adopters who want the projections machine-readable. The markdown body carries the reasoning and is deliberately unconstrained. Frontmatter is validated as JSON: normalise YAML dates to ISO-8601 strings before validation. See TDR-0009.

status.description: `stale` is the standing-loss state (spec §5.1): a stale case authorises nothing.

accountable_owner.description: A named individual — not a committee.

review_date.description: When the case must be re-examined even if no trigger fires.

disposition.description: Design-time assurance dispositions (distinct from any runtime disposition vocabulary an adopter uses).

assurance.description: Optional structured summary of the case. The body remains the reasoning; this block makes the conclusions queryable.

monitoring_contract.description: The design-time authoring of the decision's monitorable contract — see spec §5.2 item 9.

constraints.description: Non-empty when disposition is proceed-with-constraints and this block is present. The rule itself is spec §5.2 item 11; a frontmatter-only case carries its constraints in the body.

skills_run.description: Which reasoning skills ran; non-material skills are recorded as such, not silently skipped.

assumptions.description: Stated as assumptions, never as facts.

riskDelta.description: The case records the DELTA; computing the accumulated and resultant organisational position is graph work outside this standard. Declared interactions are what that graph needs.

transfers.description: Risk moved, with destination: channel, cohort, process or institution.

declared_interactions.description: Shared controls, shared identity/authority mechanisms, concentration, correlated failures, exception accumulation — the aggregation inputs (BCBS 239 lesson, applied to decisions).

riskVector.description: All ten axes plus trend, per spec §6. Never collapsed into a composite score: a single number conceals the axis that should stop the decision.

cohort_distribution.description: Descriptive axis: which customer cohorts carry the exposure, and how unevenly.

trend.description: Trend is often more important than today's absolute value.

basis.description: What the level rests on — evidence, analysis or stated judgement.

counter_hypothesis.description: The harm the hypothesis could conceal — e.g. the control works but excludes.

threshold.description: Including any maximum tolerated differential between cohorts.

trigger.description: What fires when the threshold breaches: reopen the TDR, narrow authority, suspend, redesign.

learning.description: Examined at the case's top-level review_date — no separate date field.
